checklist: Define Hash Functions and Hash Tables

states.question: What is the worst-case lookup time in a Hash Table when all keys collide?
states.explanation: If all keys collide and chain onto a single linked list, searching for a key requires traversing the entire list of N elements, taking O(N) time.

states.analogy_description: Each apartment has a box number. A hash function takes your name and tells you which mailbox slot is yours. If two people share a mailbox slot, they chain their letters (collision).

whiteboard_data.title: Hash Table Array Slots
whiteboard_data.description: Mapping keys to integer bucket indices.

states.exercise_description: Write a simple hash function `mod_hash(key, size)` that takes an integer key and table size, and returns the modulus remainder index (`key % size`).
Write a function `has_duplicates_set(lst)` that takes a list of integers and returns `True` if it contains duplicate numbers, and `False` otherwise using a Set.

states.prompt: Explain Hashing in your own words to a peer. Use an analogy from daily life.

flashcards.back: When two distinct keys produce the same hash index value.

flashcards.front: What are two collision resolution strategies?
flashcards.back: Chaining (linked lists) and Open Addressing (linear/quadratic probing).
